name: Bhindi (Okra) Curry
recipeIngredient: Fenugreek seed - half teaspoon, slightly crushed
Okra - 12 ounces (cut into approximately 1/4 inch pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the okra and remove the top and tail. Cut into about 1/4 inch pieces (or leave whole after topping and tailing if preferred).
2. Fry mustard, coriander, and fenugreek seeds in mustard oil for 2-3 minutes (cover the pan to prevent the mustard seeds from jumping around).
3. Add garlic, onion, and ginger; gently fry for about 10 more minutes.
4. Make a paste with ground spices and a little water, then add it to the pan. Fry for another 10 minutes.
5. Add okra, tomatoes, green bell pepper, and chiles. Cover and simmer very slowly until the okra is tender but not mushy (about 15-20 minutes).
6. Serves 3-4 as a main course or 5-6 as a side vegetable.
